ocr.text: Phototype II  Phototype V
Participants, n  10  6
Sex: male, female, n  2, 8  4, 2
Age (years)  45 ± 9  38 ± 11
Body mass index (kg m−2)  26 ± 4  26 ± 3
Baseline PTH (pmol L−1)a  2·3 ± 0·9  3·8 ± 1·7
Final PTH (pmol L−1)a  2·3 ± 0·7  3·1 ± 1·2
Dietary vitamin D intake week 1 (μg per day)  3·1 ± 2·7  2·6 ± 2·5
Dietary vitamin D intake week 6 (μg per day)  3·3 ± 2·6  2·0 ± 1·4
In the study, the Phototype II group consisted of 10 participants, with a sex distribution of 2 males and 8 females, while the
Phototype V group had 6 participants, comprising 4 males and 2 females. The average age for Phototype II participants was 45
± 9 years, whereas for Phototype V, it was 38 ± 11 years. Both groups had a body mass index (BMI) of 26 ± 4 kg m−2 for
Phototype II and 26 ± 3 kg m−2 for Phototype V. The minimum erythemal dose (MED) was significantly lower in Phototype II
at 37 ± 13 mJ cm−2, compared to 146 ± 64 mJ cm−2 in Phototype V. Baseline parathyroid hormone (PTH) levels were recorded
at 2.3 ± 0.9 pmol L−1 for Phototype II and 3.8 ± 1.7 pmol L−1 for Phototype V. Final PTH levels decreased to 2.3 ± 0.7 pmol
L−1 in Phototype II and were 3.1 ± 1.2 pmol L−1 in Phototype V. Dietary vitamin D intake during week 1 was 3.1 ± 2.7 μg per
day for Phototype II and 2.6 ± 2.5 μg per day for Phototype V, while week 6 intake was 3.3 ± 2.6 μg per day for Phototype II
and 2.0 ± 1.4 μg per day for Phototype V. Baseline 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) levels were 36.5 ± 13.0 nmol L−1 for
Phototype II and 17.2 ± 6.3 nmol L−1 for Phototype V, with final levels at 54.3 ± 10.5 nmol L−1 for Phototype II and 25.5 ± 9.5
nmol L−1 for Phototype V.
